items.headline: Nine new homes proposed for Old Trace Road subdivision
items.summary: The City Council heard a proposal to subdivide a 1-acre vacant lot into nine single-family homes plus seven junior accessory dwelling units on Old Trace Road. This project would add 16 new residential units to Palo Alto's housing stock.

items.headline: Summerhill redevelopment at El Camino Real moves forward
items.summary: Tree removal and replacement work has been approved for the Palo Alto Greens parcel as part of the larger Summerhill redevelopment project at 4345 El Camino Real. This signals progress on a significant mixed-use development in a key commercial corridor.

items.headline: City approves lease revenue bonds for new public safety building
items.summary: Mountain View City Council approved financing for a new public safety building project on Tuesday, April 28. This represents a major capital investment in the city's police and emergency services infrastructure.

items.headline: Council to review biodiversity and urban forest plan
items.summary: During a study session on April 28, the City Council reviewed a draft Biodiversity and Urban Forest Plan. This plan will guide how Mountain View manages its trees, green spaces, and wildlife habitat in coming years.

items.headline: Mixed-use development proposed: 67 homes plus retail in Sunnyvale
items.summary: A 4.22-acre site could see 55 townhomes, 12 apartments, and 18,535 square feet of retail space including a new two-story building, while preserving the existing Speedy's Tacos location. The Planning Commission held a study session on April 27 to review the project before making a decision.

items.headline: Campbell updates business license tax rates for fiscal year
items.summary: The City Council approved changes to business license tax rates under a revised municipal code ordinance. This annual update affects local business costs and city revenue for the coming fiscal year.

items.headline: FIFA World Cup 2026: Large promotional signs approved for Levi's Stadium area
items.summary: Santa Clara authorized temporary supergraphic signs on buildings near Levi's Stadium during the 2026 FIFA World Cup events. The signs will be permitted for a limited time period surrounding the tournament matches.

items.headline: Firefighters union contract approved for 2026-2029
items.summary: The City Council approved a three-year memorandum of understanding with the Santa Clara Firefighters union (Local 1171), covering employment terms through 2029.
